← All Digests Tuesday, March 3, 2026

Microsoft AI CEO: "18 Months" to Full Automation, Block Cuts 4,000 Jobs, AI Eats SaaS

Today's top stories in AI + Product Marketing

1

Microsoft AI CEO: Marketing Automated in 18 Months

Mustafa Suleyman made waves declaring that all white-collar jobs — including marketing — will be automated within 18 months. Bold? Yes. But the receipts are piling up: Block just cut 4,000 jobs citing "intelligence at the core," and Accenture's already trimmed 11,000 roles as AI reshapes consulting. The pace of change isn't a prediction anymore — it's a timeline.

Read on CMSWire →
2

Business Insider: AI Is Eating Software

Anthropic's Cowork plugins are turning their AI into role-specific specialists — marketing, sales, legal, finance, customer support. Instead of buying SaaS products, companies can now plug Claude into workflows directly. StackBlitz's CEO says they've built in-house AI replacements for multiple SaaS tools. When "build vs. buy" becomes "prompt vs. buy," the software moat disappears.

Read on Business Insider →
3

HubSpot: AI Agent Playbook for Marketing Automation

HubSpot released its AI predictions for 2026 alongside a new AI Agent Playbook — step-by-step frameworks for automating marketing workflows and deploying agents across campaigns. The signal: the playbook era is here. It's not about whether to use AI agents in marketing, but how fast you can operationalize them.

Read on HubSpot →
4

Four Major Ad Agencies Now Run on Claude

Four unnamed major advertising agencies are now using Anthropic's Claude enterprise tools to automate brand-related tasks — from SEO audits to client campaigns. The agencies aren't just experimenting; they're operationalizing AI across their workflows. When holding companies start standardizing on AI tools, the creative industry shifts fast.

Read on Humai Blog →
5

GenAI Trends 2026: Agentic AI + Hyperpersonalization

Ciklum's new watchlist highlights five trends reshaping enterprise tech: agentic AI, hyperpersonalization at scale, enterprise memory systems, AI workers in ops, and the rise of "AI-as-cofounder" for startups. The throughline: AI isn't just augmenting workflows — it's becoming the workflow itself.

Read on Ciklum →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The Business Insider piece on AI eating software. Anthropic's Cowork plugins are the clearest signal yet that the SaaS model faces existential pressure. When marketers can spin up custom tools via prompts instead of buying licenses, the entire competitive landscape shifts. For PMMs: this isn't just a threat to your vendors — it's an opportunity to rethink your entire stack.
